title: Byrsonima altissima
taxonomy: Byrsonima altissima is classified within the family Malpighiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the barberry family. This family is part of the order Malpighiales, which consists of approximately 130 genera and 1,800 species. The order Malpighiales is a part of the clade Rosidae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the family Malpighiaceae, Byrsonima altissima is placed in the genus Byrsonima, which is a group of plants native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The specific classification of Byrsonima altissima is further divided into the species level, which is Byrsonima altissima.

intro: Byrsonima altissima, also known as "Orellana" or "Guacatonga," is a plant species belonging to the Malpighiaceae family, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and skin conditions.
